name: Austin, William John
motto: We speak not; for silence has a language more divine.
description: He is 5 feet 6 inches high, weighs 140 pounds, has black hair and blue eyes, and was always considered, by his classmates, as one of the quietest and most studious of boys.

name: Baker, Vernon
motto: I have scarce ever met with a man who knew more or said less.
description: He was noted as a hard student and a steady, earnest worker. Marshal Commencement, '03.

name: Holman, Arthur Elliott
motto: Time is never lost that is devoted to work.
description: He is 5 feet 6 inches tall, and weighs 135 pounds. His eyes are brown and his hair light.

name: Lide, Thomas Norwood
motto: "Studious, he sat with all his books around, Sinking from thought to a vast profound."
description: He is the "kid" of our class and believes in the old adage—"An idle brain is the devil's workshop."

name: Lafar, Lipscomb.
motto: How silently serene a sea of pride! How daring an ambition! yet how deep.
clubs: Vice-President and President Calhoun Literary Society
description: Dark brown hair and gray eyes, weight 145 lbs., height 5 ft. 11 in. Early education received at Limestone College, Gaffney Male and Female Seminary, and Gaffney Graded Schools.

name: London, John Rutherford Jr.
motto: Little things sometimes have their value.
description: He was born and raised in Rock Hill and hopes to die there. He is 5 feet 1 1-2 inches high and pulls 110 pounds; his eyes are gray and his hair dark brown. John has a decidedly business head.

name: Manigault, Henry Middleton
motto: "Though gay as mirth, as curious thought sedate; As elegance polite, as power elate."
description: This animal first saw the light at the old historical town of Charleston, S. C., November 25, 1883; but was moved soon after to Summerville, S. C., where he obtained his early education—leaving this school to come to College in 1900. Henry is a light-haired, gray-eyed, tall, slender chap, standing 6 feet and weighing 145 pounds. This "Goat" was always a sturdy fellow, believing that "whatever is worth doing, is worth doing well."

name: Mauldin, Wayne Fulton
motto: Grave, chaste, contented though retired, and of all other men desired.
description: Will probably devote his time to the study of Chemistry.

name: Matthews, Hubert Willard
motto: "His poverty was glad, his heart content; Nor knew he what the spleen of vapors meant."
clubs: Secretary, Treasurer and Business Manager, Epicurean Club
description: We all know "Hube," or, at least, we think we do. This handsome, blue-eyed, brown-haired fellow might be taken for a football player (though he is not), standing 6 feet and weighing 175 pounds. "Hube" is very fond of the college girls, and has often to go to Spartanburg to consult an occupation list (?).

name: Maxwell, John
motto: He piped, he sung. And playing drove his jolly troop along.
clubs: Quarterback of the All-Southern Football Team of '03
Member of the All-Heisman football and baseball teams, '03-'04
Captain of the Baseball and Football Teams, '04
description: John's height is 5 feet 10 inches; weight 145 pounds; he has flaxen hair and blue eyes. His early education was received at the Anderson Graded School. John's most marked talents are musical and athletic.

name: McIver, George Walter
motto: Our various cares in one great point combine. The business of our lives—that is—to dine.
description: He is, however, a low countryman, as his parents soon moved to Charleston, where his early education was received. His nickname, "Tiny," is an appropriate one, he being 5 feet 5 inches in height and weighing 134 pounds; he has light hair and gray eyes. Since he "landed," in September, 1900, he has been a warm advocate of all branches of athletics, having won the "Varsity C" for tennis. Tiny was not a military man—his voice didn't suit.

name: McWhorter, Walter Marion
motto: For some who have his secret nature guessed, Have found him not too much a priest.
description: He is a little gray-eyed, dark-haired fellow, standing 5 feet 7 1/2 inches, and weighing 128 pounds. He is very fond of military life. He is a natural born mechanic, being able to split a match into twenty pieces, leaving head enough on each piece to light a cigarette.

name: Miller, Robert Earle
motto: Too little known to be appreciated; too retiring to win renown.
description: He is 6 feet above the surface and weighs 165 pounds; black hair and gray eyes. His early education was received in the public schools and graded school of Easley. He was a devout worshipper of Nature and could think and tell his thoughts in the most beautiful rhyme-won gold medals for best poems in CHRONICLE, for '01-2 and '02-3.

name: Innis, Hunton Morehead
motto: He oft our slowly growing works impart, While images reflect from art to art.
description: He is a somewhat tall, brown-eyed, dark-haired fellow, standing 5 feet 11 inches, and weighing 150 pounds. Soon after entering College, he acquired a taste for reading, and can often be seen reading the books of his favorite authors; he is also very fond of making pen sketches, and we expect some day to see him a rival of Gibson and Christie.

name: Norton, John Clarence
motto: Seest thou a man diligent in his business. He shall stand before kings.
clubs: Reporting Critic, Literary Critic and President, Columbian Society
description: He hopes to live and die in Conway. He is 5 feet 10 inches high and weighs 139 pounds 8 17-32 oz., and has hazel eyes and brown hair. Received his early education at private school in Conway.

name: Platt, Virgil Cornelius
motto: There is nothing so becomes a man as modest stillness and humility.
description: Now resides at Holly Hill, S. C. Platt is a lad of medium height and thickness; he measures 5 feet 8 inches, and weighs 151 pounds; has brown eyes and dark hair. Before coming to Clemson, in 1900, he spent his early school days at Holly Hill Graded School.

name: Lloyd, Geoffrey Preacher
motto: "Wisdom he has, and to wisdom courage; Temper to that, and unto all success."
description: "PREACH," a blue-eyed, dark-haired, and very slender chap—being 5 feet 7 inches high and weighing 115 pounds—was born at Fairfax, S. C., May 11, 1882. His early education was gotten at Fairfax Graded School and Morrison's Preparatory School. Entering college in 1900, he proceeded to join the Calhoun Society, where he distinguished himself by making one speech during his four years' course. He is an electrical student, and was a Lieutenant in Company "F." Preacher is successful in everything, including love, and from all indications will probably be the first of the class to ask "ma." Member of Epicurean.

name: Reamer, Cornelius Youmans
motto: A fat belly and a lean brain.
description: He is 5 feet 10 1-2 inches in one direction and 212 pounds in the other; brown eyes and dark brown hair. He received most of his early love at private and graded schools of Columbia.

name: Roberts, Oswald Mortimer
motto: Few hearts like his, with virtue warmed, Few heads with knowledge so informed.
clubs: member Executive Committee, S. C. Intercollegiate Oratorical Association '02-'03
Treasurer, S. C. Intercollegiate Oratorical Association '03-'04
member of Soph. Class Committee of Three in the Insurrection of '02
description: He still lives in that historic old town; 5 feet 6 1/4 inches high, and weighs 130 pounds; brown eyes and "gingerbread" red hair. His early education was received at the Ninety-Six High School. Duke's military career began brilliantly, but, in junior, came to a sudden close. He was our second 1st Sergeant when hard luck came; later, however, he was appointed Lieutenant Co. "H," and here came his honors: won

name: Tarbox, John Preston
motto: "His life was gentle and the elements so mixed in him that Nature might stand up and say to all the world, This is a man!"
clubs: Medal for best Essay in THE CHRONICLE, '02-'03
Active member of the Y. M. C. A.
Member of Columbian Society, Sergeant-at-Arms, Secretary, Treasurer, and President
description: This dark-haired, gray-eyed chap obtained his early education in the graded schools of that place. "Box," though one of the smallest of the class—standing 5 1/2 feet and weighing 110 pounds—is one of the brightest. This little fellow has been a most promising man while at Clemson, and though noted for having so much to say (?), bids fair to make his "mark in the world."

name: Templeton, William Livingston
motto: His love was passion's essence—as a tree on fire by lightning.
clubs: Marshal, Annual Celebration, Palmetto Literary Society
description: One of the Abbeville delegation. He was 5 feet 9 inches high, tipping the scales at 138 pounds; blue eyes and brown hair. He received his early learning at the Abbeville Graded School. A pinch came, and he did the clean thing and took the consequence. Won debater's medal Palmetto Society Contest, '04.

name: Thornwell, Edward Allison
motto: Blessed with a taste exact, yet unconfined; A knowledge of both books and human kind.
clubs: Literary Editor, The Clemson College Chronicle '03-'04
description: He is a youthful looking lad, measuring 5 feet 9 inches in one direction and 150 pounds in the other; has brown eyes and brown hair.

name: Brown, Earnest Furman
motto: "With thee are silent fame, Forgotten arts, and wisdom disappeared."
clubs: Corresponding Secretary, Sergeant-at-Arms, Reporting Critic, and Vice-President Columbian Literary Society
description: His present home is near Anderson city. Has brown eyes and black hair, weighs 141 1/2 lbs. and stands 5 ft. 11 in. Early education obtained at Trinity and Hopewell schools.

name: Samuel, Graham Bryan
motto: He that conquers me will find a stubborn foe.
description: Received his early education at the Graded School at Salters. His height is 5 ft. 6 1-2 in., and his weight 132 lbs. Entered Clemson with the "High Prep regime" of 1899.

name: Walker, William Pinckney
motto: Count that day lost whose low descending sun, Views from thy hand no worthy action done.
description: He is a tall "lanky" fellow, measuring 6 ft. in one direction and 135 lbs. in the other; his eyes are blue and his hair black. He expects to live and die at Monie, S. C.

name: White, Henry Stephens
motto: To him nature giveth for defence His formidable innocence.
description: He received his early education at Pinopolis Academy, and came to Clemson in September, 1900. He weighs 135 lbs. and measures 5 ft. 9 inches; has black hair and brown eyes.

name: Wier, Joel Alexander
motto: "Since this world is so rich in resplendent eyes, 'Twere a pity to confine a man's love to a pair."
description: Now resides at Ninety-Six. His early education was received at Ninety-Six High School. He weighs 146 lbs. and measures 5 ft. 10 in.; the color of his eyes is hazel, of his hair black. Joel is very popular among the ladies.

name: Williams, John Franklin
motto: Weighs well and wisely what to sell or buy; Complete in all life's lessons—but to die.
hometown: Cedar Rock, near Easley, S. C.
description: His present home is at Cedar Rock. Height 5 ft. 8 in.; weight 150 lbs.; has black hair and light blue eyes. His early education was gotten in the country schools of Pickens County and in the Graded School at Easley.

name: Connor, James Rutledge
motto: Auld Truth hersel' might swear ye're fair, And Honour safely back her, And modesty assume your air, And ne'er a one mistak' her.
clubs: Secretary Y. M. C. A., '02 and '03
Secretary, Literary Critic and President Calhoun Society
description: "RUT" is one of the low country "sand-lappers," having been born at Sand Ridge, S. C., on August 6th, 1884. From head to heels he measures 6 feet and weighs 150 pounds; has brown hair and eyes. After going to the Eutawville High School, he joined us in September, 1900. His taste inclines toward Agriculture and Chemistry, so he took the Agricultural Course. "Rut's" most noted characteristic is his modesty. Won debater's medal Calhoun Society, '04.

name: Perrin, Chiles Cothran
motto: In framing artists, art hath thus decreed; To make some good, but others to exceed.
clubs: Sergeant-at-Arms, Secretary and President, Columbian Society
description: He is of the tall, lean kind, being 5 feet 11 in. by 147 pounds; his hair is dark brown, his eyes are brown. He was one of the few who joined us in Soph., 1901—one of the youngest men in the class and a clean sleever.

name: Elias, Percy Lee
motto: Canst thou hold up thy heavy eyes awhile, And touch thy instrument a strain or two?
clubs: Corresponding Secretary, Literary Critic and Vice-President, Columbian Literary Society
description: Percy is 5 feet 9 inches high and tips the scales at 145, brown eyes and black hair. He was one of the half dozen who joined us in Soph. HYPNOTISM

name: Samuel, James Faris
motto: Heaven has allotted, soon or late, some lucky revolution of his fate.
description: His early education was drummed into him at Belmont Public School. Sam started to farm after leaving school; but seeing something better in store for him, left for college. Sam probably will be a dynamo tender, but he still has an inclination toward the old farm.

name: Felder, Samuel Ira
motto: I came here not to study, and my mission I fulfilled.
description: His early education was received at Camden Road Academy. He came to Clemson in September, 1900, to engage in the struggle for a Sheepskin. He tips the scales at 148 lbs., and measures 5 ft. 11 in.

name: Strother, Ford
motto: Wonderer at all he meets; Wonderer chiefly at himself; Who can tell him what he is?
description: Present home at Mitford. Brown eyes and black hair; stands 5 feet 10 inches and weighs 130 pounds. Early education received at Mitford.

name: Gandy, Ralph Albert
motto: He struggles with his lot through toilsome years, Kept to his task by daily want of bread.
description: This lightning wielder was born at Society Hill, S. C., on August 24, 1881. Present residence at Lumber, S. C. Has brown eyes and dark hair, weighs 150 lbs., and is 5 ft. 11 in. high. Early education received at Society Hill and Darlington, S. C.

name: Gelzer, John Jr.
motto: Friendship is constant in all other things, Save in the office and in the affairs of love.
description: "Jack" is a tall, slender lad, with black hair and brown eyes, and his total 5 ft. 11 inches pulls the scales only for 148 lbs.

name: Molten, Alexander Grace
motto: "Young man with aught, but elegance dispense, Maturer years require a little sense!"
description: His early education was received at the public schools of Waterloo and at Porter's Military Academy. He is a blue-eyed, light-haired, well-built fellow, standing 5 ft. 8 3-4 inches, and weighing 152 pounds. Entering Soph. in 1900, he took the Electrical Course, but was taken sick in 1903 and left College. He joined our class in Senior. Molten is very fond of the girls, or rather they are fond of him, since he is so cute, and we, therefore, predict that he will never marry.

name: Hall, Vernon Barber
motto: Nature is his soul's high priest, And his temple, space.
clubs: Literary Editor, THE CLEMSON COLLEGE CHRONICLE
Exchange Editor, THE CLEMSON COLLEGE CHRONICLE
member, Committee of Three in Soph. Insurrection
description: Hall is a rather slender chap, with blue eyes and brown hair; stands 5 feet 11 inches, and weighs 145 pounds. Although he is pursuing Electrical Engineering as an avocation, yet his inclinations seem to be decidedly literary. Although not noted for militaryism.

name: Hamlin, Frank Thompson
motto: So long shalt last thy unmolested reign, Nor any dare to take thy name in vain.
description: Present home at Anderson, S. C. Dark brown hair and brown eyes; weighs 150 pounds; stands 5 feet 11 inches. Early education received at Anderson Graded School and Patrick Military Institute.

name: Andrew, Mcilwain Henry
motto: Foremost captain of his time, rich in saving common sense, And, as the greatest only are, in his simplicity sublime.
clubs: Censor, Secretary, Literary Critic, Vice-President and President Palmetto Society
description: He is 5 ft. 8 1/2 in. high, and weighs 148 pounds. He was always one of the most popular men in his class.

name: Hill, James Monchausen
motto: So when thou shalt come into these barren years, thou mayst not bring a mind unfurnished and a withered heart.
description: With a head covered with dark brown hair, and lighted by two smiling blue eyes, he makes a soldierly appearance, as he presents his 150 avoirdupois with an altitudinal dimension of 5 feet 8 inches. Won Board of Trustees' medal in June, '03, as the best drilled cadet in the corps.

name: Hill, Samuel Thomson
motto: Happy am I, from care I am free, Why ain't they all contented like me.
clubs: College representative at South Carolina Intercollegiate Oratorical Contest, Greenwood, S. C.
Literary Critic and President Palmetto Society
description: He weighs 138 pounds, is 5 ft. 9 1/2 inches tall, has blue eyes and black hair. After receiving his early education at the Abbeville public schools, he became a Freshman in September, 1900, and took the Electrical Course. He won the "Moise Medal" for oratory in the Palmetto Society, '03.
